title: Sprint 1 Planning
summary: Kickoff meeting to define sprint 1 backlog, agree on 2-week sprint cadence, and assign initial work streams for onboarding and dashboard.
keyPoints: Defined initial sprint backlog with 8 stories
Agreed on 2-week sprint cadence
Alex and James to collaborate on onboarding flow first
decisions: Sprint cadence set to 2 weeks with standups on Mon/Wed/Fri

title: Architecture Decision: Database Choice
summary: Evaluated Postgres vs MongoDB. Maria's benchmarks showed Postgres handles our query patterns better. Tom confirmed infrastructure compatibility.
keyPoints: Evaluated Postgres vs MongoDB for primary data store
Maria presented benchmarks showing Postgres handles our query patterns better
Tom confirmed Postgres fits existing infrastructure tooling
decisions: Selected PostgreSQL as primary database — better fit for relational task data and existing team expertise

title: Design Review: Onboarding Flow
summary: James presented 5-screen onboarding mockups. Ben pushed for 3 screens for faster time-to-value. Team agreed on progressive disclosure approach with A/B testing.
keyPoints: James presented 5-screen onboarding flow mockups
Ben requested reducing to 3 screens for faster time-to-value
Alex confirmed feasibility of progressive disclosure approach
Agreed to A/B test short vs long onboarding in beta
decisions: Approved 3-screen onboarding flow with progressive disclosure

title: Infrastructure Planning
summary: Mapped CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions with automated staging deploys. Defined environment promotion strategy from dev through staging to production.
keyPoints: Mapped out CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions to staging to production
Maria requested dedicated test database for integration tests
Defined environment promotion strategy: dev → staging → prod
decisions: GitHub Actions selected for CI/CD with automated staging deploys on merge to main

title: Bug Triage Session
summary: Reviewed 12 open bugs from internal testing. Prioritised 3 critical auth flow bugs as P0 and mobile layout issues as P1.
keyPoints: Reviewed 12 open bugs from first week of internal testing
3 critical bugs in auth flow identified
Mobile responsive issues affecting onboarding and dashboard
decisions: Auth bugs prioritized as P0, mobile layout as P1

audioReference.notes: Recording exists in Drive; written summary not yet finalised.

title: Scope Negotiation
summary: Established strict v1 feature boundary to protect launch date. Deferred advanced reporting, team collaboration, and calendar integration to v1.1.
keyPoints: Lisa shared beta user perspective on essential features
Agreed to defer advanced reporting to v1.1
Established strict v1 feature boundary to protect launch date
decisions: Advanced reporting, team collaboration, and calendar integration deferred to v1.1
Beta feedback to be formally categorized as v1 must-have vs v1.1 nice-to-have

title: Beta Feedback Debrief
summary: Both beta testers rated onboarding positively. Mike flagged mobile layout issues on Android. Lisa requested keyboard shortcuts. No decisions made — feedback logged for triage.
keyPoints: Both beta testers rated onboarding positively (4+ out of 5)
Mike reported mobile layout issues on Android devices
Lisa requested keyboard shortcuts for power users
Sarah presented aggregated survey results

title: Auth Bug Post-Mortem
summary: Identified root cause of auth failures: OAuth token refresh fails on non-standard provider error format. 3 critical edge cases remain. Auth fix declared a launch blocker.
keyPoints: Root cause identified: OAuth token refresh fails when provider returns non-standard error format
3 critical edge cases in auth flow still failing in QA
Auth provider had 2 outages in March affecting our integration
Need fallback auth mechanism before launch
decisions: Auth flow fix is launch blocker — no launch until all 3 edge cases resolved

title: Stakeholder Update
summary: Progress review: 4 of 15 actions complete, 5 in progress, 3 blocked. Auth blockers may push launch by 1 week. Load testing added to priority list.
keyPoints: 4 of 15 actions complete, 5 in progress, 3 blocked
Auth blockers may push launch date by 1 week
Load testing not yet started — added to priority list

title: Launch Checklist Review
summary: Reviewed 15-item launch checklist. Auth flow and SSL certificates are blocking. Launch date formally at risk — reassessment scheduled for April 15.
keyPoints: Reviewed 15-item launch checklist
Auth flow and SSL certificates are blocking items
Load testing must complete before launch approval
Error monitoring setup still pending
decisions: Launch date formally at risk — team to reassess on April 15
